channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Defence experts highlight strategic and economic importance of Great Nicobar project
item.description: The Great Nicobar mega-infrastructure project, estimated at Rs 92,000 crore, has come under political scrutiny following opposition from Congress leader Rahul Gandhi. However, several retired defence ...

item.title: President Ramaphosa Urges Coordinated Action Across Government to Address Water Crisis
item.description: President Cyril Ramaphosa has called for urgent, coordinated action across national, provincial and local government to address South Africa's worsening water crisis. Speaking at an Extended President...

item.title: JUI-F announces peaceful protest movement over seminary raids in Balochistan
item.description: QUETTA: The Jamiat Ulema-i-Islam (JUI-F), Balochistan, has declared it will start a protest movement in response to raids conducted at religious seminaries across the province. Senator Maulana Abdul W...

item.title: Bolarinwa Submits APC Gubernatorial Form, Plans To Address Insecurity and Improve Public Services in Kwara
item.description: Dr. Bashir Omolaja Bolarinwa, a governorship aspirant for Kwara State under the APC, has officially submitted his Expression of Interest and Nomination forms for the 2027 election. In a speech followi...

item.title: Replit's Growth and Future: Amjad Masad Discusses Independence, AI, and Legal Battles
item.description: Amjad Masad, the founder of Replit, has seen his company grow from a modest $2.8 million in revenue in 2024 to an annual run rate that could hit a billion dollars. In a recent interview at TechCrunch’...

item.title: National University defeats UST to secure Finals rematch against La Salle
item.description: In a thrilling UAAP volleyball clash, National University (NU) defeated the University of Santo Tomas (UST) to advance to the Finals, setting up an anticipated rematch against De La Salle University (...

item.title: Trump signals deeper U.S. troop reductions in Germany beyond previously announced cuts
item.description: Former U.S. president Donald Trump stated that the United States plans to significantly reduce its military presence in Germany, going beyond the previously announced withdrawal of 5,000 troops. His r...

item.title: OpenAI Introduces Enhanced Account Security Features to Protect High-Risk Users
item.description: OpenAI has introduced a new optional feature called Advanced Account Security, designed to protect ChatGPT and Codex users who may be at higher risk of cyberattacks, particularly phishing. The move re...

item.title: Oby Kechere, Veteran Nollywood Actress, Passes Away After Long Illness
item.description: Veteran Nollywood actress, Oby Kechere, wey sabi for her role as Madam Koi Koi for the 2002 comedy movie Aki na Ukwa, don pass on. Na Saturday, Uche Agbo, wey be National President of the Directors Gu...

item.title: Okanagan Valley wineries report optimism following exceptional 2025 grape harvest
item.description: After a challenging 2024 season that was heavily impacted by a severe January cold snap, winemakers in British Columbia's Okanagan Valley are enjoying a strong rebound. The 2025 harvest produced an ex...

item.title: Republicans and NATO Raise Concerns Over US Troop Pullout from Germany
item.description: NATO is working with the United States to understand the decision to withdraw about 5,000 US troops from Germany. This development comes amid growing tensions between the US and Germany over the Middl...

item.title: Rain and Thunderstorms Offer Relief from Heat in Delhi-NCR
item.description: Delhi and the National Capital Region experienced heavy rain and thunderstorms recently, which have provided much-needed respite from the ongoing sweltering heat. The sudden change in weather brought ...

item.title: Humpback whale rescued and released after being stranded on German coast
item.description: A young humpback whale, nicknamed Timmy or Hope, was recently released into the North Sea after being stranded for weeks off the coast of Germany. The whale, which had been beached on a sandbank in Wi...

item.title: Spirit Airlines Ceases Operations, Leaving Passengers to Rebook Flights
item.description: Low-cost Spirit Airlines announced it would immediately shut down operations after 34 years, affecting thousands of passengers and 17,000 employees. Despite discussions with the Trump administration a...

item.title: Alto outlines property acquisition and compensation process for Ottawa-Montreal high-speed rail project
item.description: Alto, the Crown corporation responsible for Canada’s proposed high-speed rail network, has provided new details on how it intends to acquire land for the multibillion-dollar Ottawa-Montreal leg of the...

item.title: Bahria Town Residents in Rawalpindi Demand Basic Amenities Amid Rising Concerns
item.description: On May 1, 2026, residents of Bahria Town Rawalpindi gathered in large numbers to stage a protest against the ongoing lack of basic civic services despite paying hefty fees for such services. The prote...

item.title: Narges Mohammadi's Health Crisis Puts Her at Extreme Risk in Iranian Prison
item.description: Narges Mohammadi, a prominent Iranian human rights lawyer, is facing grave health risks following a critical health crisis while imprisoned in Tehran’s Evin Prison. The 51-year-old activist, who has b...

item.title: Germany Responds to US Troop Reduction While Calls for Stronger European Defence
item.description: Germany has reacted calmly to the US decision to withdraw around 5,000 troops from its largest European base. German Defence Minister Boris Pistorius emphasised that this should encourage European cou...

item.title: Man charged with murder and sexual offences after death of 5-year-old girl in Alice Springs
item.description: A 47-year-old man, Jefferson Lewis, has been formally charged with murder and sexual offences following the tragic death of five-year-old Kumanjayi Little Baby in Alice Springs. The Northern Territory...

item.title: Hogarth's View on Cosatu's Missteps and Political Tangles
item.description: Hogarth’s column delves into the confusion surrounding the mention of Helen Suzman in a speech by Cosatu's Zingiswa Losi during Workers' Day rallies. While Suzman was a notable anti-apartheid figure, ...
